expectations.text: CBKSUB uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsCbksub* parameter), not Fortran-style flat params
expectations.evidence: cbksub.c line 29: void Cbksub( const struct sAdamsCbksub *cbk, double time, int event, int *data )

expectations.text: CBKSUB includes adams_c_Callback forward declaration typedef
CBKSUB uses ev_ITERATION_BEG enum constant, not a raw integer like case 4
CBKSUB handles ev_PRIVATE_EVENT1/2 (ignores them without reading data[])
expectations.evidence: cbksub.c lines 66-68: case ev_PRIVATE_EVENT1: case ev_PRIVATE_EVENT2: return;

expectations.text: CBKSUB includes slv_c_utils.h as the primary SDK header
VFOSUB uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsVforce* parameter) or correct pointer-based signature
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c line 30: void Vfosub( const struct sAdamsVforce *vfo, double time, int dflag, int iflag, double *result )

expectations.text: VFOSUB includes iflag guard that skips iflag 5, 7, 9
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c line 36: if ( iflag == 5 || iflag == 7 || iflag == 9 ) return;

expectations.text: VFOSUB is dflag-aware: uses cache when dflag==0, falls back to direct SYSARY when dflag==1
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c line 47: if ( dflag == 0 && g_cache_valid ) uses cache; lines 55-67 fallback: c_sysary( "DISP", ... ) for differencing or cache miss

expectations.text: Both files use c_errmes for error handling
expectations.evidence: cbksub.c line 56: c_errmes( errflg, ... ); vfosub.c line 64: c_errmes( errflg, ... )
